verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (p24δ1, also known as p24) is a type I single-pass transmembrane protein of the p24/TMED family. It has a large N-terminal luminal/extracellular domain and a single TM helix. It primarily cycles between ER and Golgi as a cargo receptor, but it also traffics to and is stably present at the plasma membrane where its ectodomain is exposed on the outer leaflet. Surface biotinylation and proteomics studies have documented TMED10 at the cell surface. It also functions as a receptor for prion protein and amyloid precursor protein at the PM, consistent with a genuine surface pool.
